source_gate.manual_precision_audit_result: official field extraction was plausible in sampled HSR detail pages, but mapping precision to event-date liquid U.S. common stocks was not auditable because no durable mapper/event table was built
source_gate.timestamp_safety_assessment: date-only FTC HSR notices can be next-session mapped conservatively but do not provide verified first-public timestamps per notice; DOJ detail pages may expose article:published_time metadata but a combined official event table was not built
source_gate.rejection_reason: failed source gate: no durable official event table with >=150 conservatively mapped liquid U.S. common-stock events, source URLs/IDs, timestamp-safe next-session mapping, role labels, dropped-event logs, manual mapping precision audit, and concentration diagnostics

official_source_samples.mapping_note: Global-E may be public/foreign and Passport appears private/subsidiary-like; not accepted without event-date security mapping
public-company name present, but fund acquirer and acquired-entity/subsidiary fields require dedupe and point-in-time role/security mapping before use
official timestamp exists on sample detail page, but no broad DOJ merger-enforcement event table was constructed

failure_modes_seen: FTC HSR API endpoint returns 403 without an API key despite reachable documentation
FTC HSR detail pages are date-only for the notice event; page markup also contains unrelated site times that must be ignored
simple FTC press search for merger is noisy and duplicated on the first page
DOJ base pages are reachable but one DOJ search query returned 403
HSR parties are frequently private, funds, partnerships, subsidiaries, foreign issuers, or legal-name variants requiring point-in-time issuer/security mapping
no compact dropped-event log, dedupe, concentration diagnostics, or >=150 liquid U.S. common-stock mappings were produced
